_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
Tuesday March 10th | 3:22

Bonjour Tremblant,

Given the particularly unfavorable weather conditions expected overnight, Tremblant ski mountain, equipment rentals, and the Snow School will be closed on Wednesday, March 11.

The safety of our guests and employees is always our top priority. As a result, all skiing and snowboarding activities, equipment rentals, and Snow School programs will be suspended for the day.

Our teams will be closely monitoring weather conditions and working to prepare the mountain for a safe reopening as soon as possible. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may cause and appreciate your understanding.

We look forward to welcoming you back on the slopes very soon.

Mountain safety tip of the week: Slow down in reduced‑speed zones.
Reduced‑speed areas mean it’s time to slow down for your safety and the safety of others.
Remember, you’re sharing the slopes with other skiers and riders. Stay cautious at all times.
Some zones marked in yellow on the trail map require reduced speed. Please be considerate, and ski or ride slowly in these areas out of respect for the higher traffic.

Snow fall is recorded daily at 5 a.m from mid-mountain at North Side (550 m / 1,800 ft). This data, based on 24 hours, provides a picture of on-mountain conditions. Actual accumulation may differ from the official report. Be aware of changing conditions. Lifts and trails exist in a mountain environment and may close due to weather, usage and/or special maintenance.
_inspectorSnowReport.Alert:
Mountain closure on March 11

Given the particularly unfavorable weather conditions expected overnight, Tremblant ski mountain, equipment rentals, and the Snow School will be closed on Wednesday, March 11.

_inspectorSnowReport.SafetyReportFrench: En raison des conditions météorologiques particulièrement défavorables prévues au cours de la nuit, la montagne de ski Tremblant, les locations d’équipement ainsi que l’École de neige seront fermées le mercredi 11 mars.
